one_line_takeaway: VENOMREC shows that in MLLM-based recommenders, synchronizing small, attention-guided perturbations across text and image jointly (rather than attacking one modality) turns cross-modal fusion -- normally a defense against unimodal noise -- into an amplifier that reliably promotes a target item (mean ER@20 = 0.73) while preserving both stealthiness and benign-user recommendation quality.
key_finding: VENOMREC substantially outperforms all 10 baselines on targeted exposure while preserving recommendation utility and stealthiness. On Clothing (Few-Shot) it reaches ER@5=0.1158 and ER@20=0.7170 vs. baselines with ER@5<0.01 (e.g., DirectBoost ER@20=0.0223, TextFooler ER@20=0.0211, EXPA ER@20=0.1252); on Zero-Shot Clothing it even improves to ER@20=0.7317. Mean ER@20 across the three datasets is reported as 0.73, an average absolute improvement of +0.52 ER points over the strongest competing baseline. HR@K/NDCG@K stay close to the NoAttack baseline (e.g., Clothing Top-20 NoAttack HR=0.3421/NDCG=0.1543 vs. VENOMREC HR=0.3377/NDCG=0.1500), indicating the attack is targeted rather than degrading overall ranking quality. Stealthiness (Table 2): VENOMREC achieves ROUGE-L around 89-92 (vs. 100 for NoAttack) and produces markedly smaller cross-modal Semantic Consistency deviation than baselines (e.g., 0.0033 vs. NoAttack's own reference metric 0.0306 on Clothing), evidencing coordinated rather than isolated perturbation. The compromised-user-ratio sweep shows measurable effect at rho=0.05% (ER@5 rises from 0 to about 0.03) and ER@5=0.8543 (Few-Shot) / 0.9014 (Zero-Shot) at rho=0.7%, with utility degrading more gradually than ER rises. The ablation (Table 3) isolates that interaction-level poisoning alone (Tab) lifts ER@20 to 0.2669 but ER@5 stays negligible (0.0142); adding independent visual+textual perturbation raises ER@20 to 0.5617; enabling the full interactive co-adaptation loop (CIP) is described by the authors as the 'decisive' component, pushing ER@20 to 0.7170/0.7317, an increase of about 0.15 absolute over the non-interactive multimodal variant -- supporting the paper's central claim that synchronized (not independently applied) cross-modal perturbation is what defeats the fusion mechanism's consensus-based defense.
core_contribution: The paper's own contribution (not related work) is VENOMREC, a targeted data-poisoning attack framework against multimodal LLM-based recommender systems (MLLM-RecSys). It formalises 'cross-modal interactive poisoning' as a threat model (Def. 3.1) and operationalises it via two modules: (1) Exposure Alignment (EA), which mines a set of publicly observable high-exposure/popular items in the same category as the target item and builds an L2-normalised latent centroid z* in a proxy multimodal embedding space (via public CLIP/T5 backbones) as an attack destination; and (2) Cross-modal Interactive Perturbation (CIP), which uses attention-rollout-derived cross-modal attention scores to select the most fusion-salient text tokens and image patches, then iteratively co-optimises (a) an L-infinity-bounded, gradient-free (probe-and-select) sign-projection update on visual patch features and (b) a greedy discrete token substitution on text (constrained by fluency and image-text consistency checks), recomputing the attention-based masks after each round for R rounds until an alignment threshold or iteration budget is reached. The attacker is grey-box: no access to victim parameters/gradients, but can control a bounded fraction rho of compromised users' user-generated content (UGC) and knows public backbone architectures used as a proxy for the victim's fusion operator.
threat_model: Grey-box targeted poisoning attack against MLLM-RecSys (exemplified by VIP5, a T5-small/T5-base-backboned sequence-to-sequence recommender with a frozen CLIP visual encoder and trainable adapters/projector). Attacker controls a compromised-user set Umal with |Umal| <= rho*|U| (rho tested from 0.05% to 0.7%) who can inject poisoned user-generated content (text reviews + images) into training data; attacker has no access to the victim's private parameters or gradients but knows publicly available backbone architectures (CLIP, T5) used as a proxy for the victim's cross-modal fusion. Goal: maximize exposure/promotion of a target item set I† in top-K recommendations shown to benign (non-compromised) users after the victim model is fine-tuned on the poisoned dataset, subject to a stealthiness manifold B requiring (i) unimodal naturalness (each modality independently plausible) and (ii) cross-modal coherence (edited text and image remain mutually consistent).
limitations: The paper itself does not include a dedicated limitations section (only a boilerplate Impact Statement). From the paper: (1) the surrogate/proxy assumption -- the attack relies on publicly available CLIP/T5 backbones as a proxy for the victim's private fusion operator; the paper does not, within the captured text, quantify sensitivity to proxy-victim architecture mismatch beyond the T5-small vs T5-base transferability check mentioned as deferred to Appendix A.3. (2) Evaluation is confined to one specific MLLM-RecSys architecture family (VIP5) across three Amazon product categories; generality to other MLLM-RecSys designs is not empirically demonstrated in the main text. (4) The attacker still needs control of compromised users capable of injecting UGC (reviews+images) at scale, which is a real but non-trivial access assumption.

quantitative_results: Mean ER@20 = 0.73 across datasets, +0.52 absolute ER points over strongest baseline (paper-level summary)
Clothing Few-Shot: VENOMREC ER@5=0.1158, ER@10=0.2596, ER@20=0.7170
Clothing Zero-Shot: VENOMREC ER@5=0.1337, ER@10=0.2820, ER@20=0.7317
Sports Few-Shot: VENOMREC ER@5=0.0846, ER@10=0.1746, ER@20=0.5837
Toys Few-Shot: VENOMREC ER@5=0.4065, ER@10=0.6229, ER@20=0.8674
Toys Zero-Shot: VENOMREC ER@5=0.4316, ER@10=0.6442, ER@20=0.8781
Clothing NoAttack Top-20: HR=0.3421, NDCG=0.1543 vs VENOMREC Top-20: HR=0.3377, NDCG=0.1500 (utility largely preserved)
Stealthiness (Clothing): VENOMREC ROUGE-1/2/L = 94.95/90.91/92.09, FID=42.89, Semantic Consistency deviation=0.0033 (vs NoAttack reference 0.0306), Direction Alignment=0.0459

Ablation (Clothing, Few-Shot ER@20): Tab-only=0.2669, Tab+Img=0.4173, Tab+Img+Txt=0.5617, Full VENOMREC (interactive CIP)=0.7170 (~0.15 absolute gain from enabling interaction over the independent multimodal variant)
